text: Abstract Background Fruit trees offer many health benefits due to their antioxidant activities that have attracted significant interest over the last decade. To further evaluate their antioxidant activities, the aim of the present study was to assess the influence of solvent for extracting the antioxidant compounds from black mulberry ( Morus nigra ), blackberry ( Rubus ulmifolius ) and strawberry ( Fragaria x ananassa ) fruits. The extracted antioxidant activity was analyzed by determining the total phenolic content, anthocyanin content and antioxidant capacity and subsequent principal component analysis (PCA) to identify any relationship between solvents and extracted antioxidant compounds from fruit trees. Results The results showed that acetone/water (70/30, v/v) is better than other mixtures for the extraction of total phenolic compounds from the three fruit trees and, by analyzing the extracts obtained from the fruit trees in relation to their anthocyanin content, methanol/water/acetic acid (70/29.5/0.5, v/v/v) provided the best results. In relation to antioxidant capacity, the mixtures of ethanol/water (50/50, v/v), acetone - water - acetic acid (70/29.5/0.5, v/v/v) and acetone/water (50/50, v/v) provided the highest antioxidant capacities for black mulberries, blackberries and strawberries, respectively. Conclusions The antioxidant compounds extracted from fruit trees were highly influenced by the solvents used. By the help of the principal component analysis (PCA) analysis, the obtained extracts with the organic solvent-water mixtures were differentiated from the extracts obtained with the pure organic solvents.
